statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

articles.headline: Steve Kerr, Doc Rivers join 'political interference' letter

articles.headline: How every five-star men's college basketball commit fits at his new school
articles.description: Breaking down top class of 2026 commitments, including a third five-star recruit for BYU in three years.

images.name: Bruce Branch III [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruce Branch III, the former No. 2 prospect who reclassified up from the 2027 class, has committed to BYU, giving Kevin Young a third consecutive class featuring a five-star recruit.

images.name: Jordan Smith [1296x729]
images.caption: Jordan Smith is the second five-star recruit of the 2026 high school class to commit to Arkansas.

images.name: Cameron Williams commit fit
images.caption: A top-3 recruit in the class of 2026, Cameron Williams is headed to Durham to play for Jon Scheyer and the Duke Blue Devils.

images.name: Jason Crowe [1144x644]
images.caption: Jason Crowe, No. 4 recruit in the 2026 ESPN 100 player rankings, has committed to Missouri.

images.name: Babatunde Oladotun [1066x600]
images.caption: Babatunde Oladotun, the former 2027 No. 1 prospect who reclassified to the 2026 class and is ranked in the top-10, has announced his commitment to Maryland.

images.name: Dylan Mingo [1296x729]
images.caption: Premier class of 2026 guard Dylan Mingo has committed to North Carolina for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Caleb Gaskins [1296x729]
images.caption: Caleb Gaskins is staying in-state next season to play for Miami and first-time head coach Jai Lucas.

images.name: Deron Rippey Jr. [1296x729]
images.caption: Deron Rippey Jr. has committed to Duke, giving the Blue Devils another top recruit.

images.name: Toni Bryant (commitment photo) [600x338]
images.caption: Toni Bryant, the No. 14 recruit in the class of 2026, has committed to Missouri, giving the Tigers two five-star prospects for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Austin Goosby (Texas) [1296x729]
images.caption: Austin Goosby has committed to Sean Miller and Texas for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Taylen Kinney [1296x729]
images.caption: Taylen Kinney is one of the best point guards in the boys' SC Next class of 2026, and recently committed to Kansas.
images.credit: Brian Rothmuller/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

article.headline: Dak's career-high 22 points and career-high 10 rebounds help Colorado withstand Utah in 92-78 win
article.description: — Bangot Dak scored a career-high 22 points and grabbed 10 rebounds and Colorado withstood Utah's second-half shooting barrage to beat the Utes 92-78 on Tuesday.
article.linkText: Dak's career-high 22 points and career-high 10 rebounds help Colorado withstand Utah in 92-78 win

images.name: Colorado Buffaloes vs. Utah Utes: Game Highlights
images.caption: Colorado Buffaloes vs. Utah Utes: Game Highlights

video.headline: Colorado Buffaloes vs. Utah Utes: Game Highlights
video.shortHeadline: Colorado Buffaloes vs. Utah Utes: Game Highlights
video.caption: Colorado Buffaloes vs. Utah Utes: Game Highlights
video.title: Colorado Buffaloes vs. Utah Utes: Game Highlights
video.description: Colorado Buffaloes vs. Utah Utes: Game Highlights
video.shortDescription: Colorado Buffaloes vs. Utah Utes: Game Highlights

tracking.trackingName: NCB_One-Play (Colorado Buffaloes vs. Utah Utes: Game Highlights) 2026/03/04 ESHEET

article.story: SALT LAKE CITY -- — Bangot Dak scored a career-high 22 points and grabbed 10 rebounds and Colorado withstood Utah's second-half shooting barrage to beat the Utes 92-78 on Tuesday. It was the 7-foot Dak's fifth double-double of the season and second this year against Utah. Isaiah Johnson also scored 22 points, Barrington Hargress scored 19 points and Jalin Holland 11 for Colorado (17-13, 7-10 Big 12). Terrence Brown scored 26 points and Don McHenry 19 for Utah (10-20, 2-15). A 13-13 contest with 14:46 left before halftime became one-sided as the Buffs went on to dominate and posted a 51-26 lead at halftime, shooting 67.9% (19 of 28) before intermission. Utah meanwhile shot 34.5% (10 of 29) and missed all nine 3-point attempts. The Utes turned it around after the break and shot 73.9% (17 of 23) including 85.7% (6 of 7) from 3-point range and got back in it. McHenry's three-point play brought Utah within 69-60 with 8:58 remaining. Colorado responded with Holland making a pair of foul shots, Dak made four straight and then a tip-in layup and Colorado stayed ahead by double digits the rest of the way. Up Next Colorado: Hosts No. 2 Arizona in a regular season finale on Saturday. Utah: Ends the regular season at Baylor on Saturday. ------ Get poll alerts and updates on the AP Top 25 throughout the season. Sign up here and here (AP News mobile app). AP college basketball: https://apnews.com/hub/ap-top-25-college-basketball-poll and https://apnews.com/hub/college-basketball
